steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to compare the sizes of the two mountain ranges. The relevant data is 1,141 mi² for El Carmen-Verde and 63 mi² for the Sierrita Mountains.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Sky islands in northwestern Mexico and the southwestern US can vary in size.’: Does not compare these two ranges using actual figures. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In terms of area, the El Carmen-Verde Mountains (which cover 1,141 mi 2) are larger than the Sierrita Mountains (63 mi 2).’: Puts both measurements side-by-side, directly achieving the comparison goal. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘There are dozens of isolated mountain ranges located in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico.’: No comparison possible without figures. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The El Carmen-Verde Mountains and the Sierrita Mountains are both isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands.’: Off-topic; substitutes a definition for the required size comparison. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
